All right. Pretty good stuff, right? Amazing. I know it is. I mean, I love this mantra myself because I truly believe that for you and for me that in the absence of having a compelling vision, you can't achieve success at anywhere near that awesome level you're capable of. You get it? Okay. Let's roll forward. Here's the deal. I'm going to hear my vision about how all of this started, what it was like when I first got out of jail and how I was able to use this exact competency of vision focus to essentially pull myself up on my bootstraps and build this incredible life, which is now, wow, taking me all over the world and places I never imagined possible. So I think you're really getting a lot of value out of this. It'll be cool for you to watch, but also you'll see what happens when you actually hit your vision. What do you do then? What's the next step? That's what you'll see here. And after you're done, I'll meet you on the other side. John, I'd like to go back to the idea of the vision focus and get some real practical examples of maybe what your personal vision looked like, just to make it real for these guys watching what kind of things did you actually conjure up? We know it's not about just sending my kids to the right school and I'm going to earn lots of money. There was a high level, wooly kind of ideas. I want to know the specifics of your vision, what it looked like so that people have got something to model against. So let's look at my most recent vision in this chapter of my life. I had a very complicated life. So this last vision, let's say what happened when I wrote these books, I wrote the books, The Wolf of Wall Street and Catching the Wolf of Wall Street. So after I wrote those books, and I was going to figure at that point my vision was to become a full-time author and just write books and kind of live on the beach somewhere and just have fun, be with my partner and make a lot of money as an author and just live the bohemian life. I was almost kidding myself at that point because I was still sort of coming to terms of what had happened back when I owned my brokerage firm and all the press and notoriety. And then the movie, I just sold the rights to Caprio and Scorsese. So there's all this stuff that was out of percolating in my life. And I think on some level it was almost fantasy to think that I would end up living my life in anonymity as an author and never speak again because my passion was always speaking. I loved speaking. I loved communicating with people. It turned me on versus writing. I hated. I absolutely struggled as an author. I would sit there and just, I felt alone and disconnected because speaking is a communication strategy and writing is a communication strategy. They're both strategies for communicating a message. The difference is that when you are writing, you're communicating to a page. The page then goes out and the people get to read it after the fact, which can be very, very powerful. People, you know, the written word is very powerful and it can, you know, go to so many people and, you know, over so many years, it's almost permanent once it's in writing. But there's no energy exchange back from the people that are looking at me as I'm, imagine if I'm writing and they're reading, oh, good job. It would be a bit more of an energy exchange and more empowering to me. So I found it almost to be stifling as an author. Right? Eventually I said, you know, I just, I'm not happy. I'm not, I don't feel fulfilled. And I threw three, let's just say three or four different evolutions of, you know, what I wanted to do with my life. I said, you know what? I know what I want to do. I said, I want to be a speaker. I want to go around the world and spread the message. I want to spread the message of how you become wealthy and successful. How do you do that without sacrificing ethics and integrity? How do you go out and become a world-class closer? Which I, you know, taught so many people to do. And I felt on some level that I taught them how to do it and they all became world-class closers. But I missed the ethics part of the equation. And I was desperate to essentially do it again and do it right this time. To teach people a skill set that would make them wealthy for the rest of their lives. Yet to make sure they also got the other end of the equation that success without ethics and integrity isn't successful. And I missed it. I simply just missed it. I was young and, you know, fueled by instant gratification and a couple of various drugs that I took back then. Right? So I'm so 15 years now. So I'm a different guy. But back then, you know, I had a lot of things circulating around my body in the form of, you know, pharmaceutical substances. Right? So sort of the guilt and remorse are quiet. And I was living a life of pure insanity. So all that sort of stuff to reconcile, you know, and I had a gift. No doubt. I had the ability to speak to people in a way where I could captivate people in person and get on stage. And I loved it. It was something that I truly loved. So I said, that's what I'm going to do. I'm going to be the world's most successful speaker. And I actually sat down and did step one and I wrote out a vision for my future. And I said, I see myself in five years standing on a stage, speaking to 3,000 people and the people will be clapping and cheering and they'll be laughing. They'll be feeling great because they feel empowered and I'll be teaching them how to make money the right. And I also said, like, I'll be teaching them how to make money the right way. There'll be ethics and integrity and I'll be empowering people all over the world to improve their lives and secure their children's futures. And as I'm doing that, the money will be pouring in because I'm giving so much value to them and I'll take that money. I'll buy myself a beautiful house on the beach. I'll be back on the beach again. I'll meet the woman of my love. She's going to have blonde hair. Have this. I wrote out the hair call because I was single back then. I wrote out the perfect woman for myself. I see exactly how she looks. She's going to be kind-hearted. She'll stick with me through thick and thin. She's not going to bolt when I get in trouble like the last one did. Off topic. But that's how detailed the vision was. I visualized the woman I wanted. I visualized myself standing on stage. I wrote what I meant and I'll be talking about the straight line system and how to be ethical and how to visualize and how to manage your state and all this stuff. Then from there, the people that are giving me referrals and I'll start working at the biggest corporations and I'll start working for governments and I wrote all this sort of stuff that just seemed so incredibly wacky. If you had read it, you'd say, I'm going to make $10 million a year and this is back when I basically had no money. It was really powerful. I'll be driving this car. I'm living in that house on the beach. Well, guess what? The problem I had is the business took off so fast that within three years I had succeeded my own vision. It all happened. Literally, all of it happened. My kids moved back in with me. I ended up getting my ex-wife's house where I used to go there when I was in trouble and she had this giant house. I moved in there. She had a financial setback. I ended up having my kids back in my life every single day. I was on stage speaking with Tony Blair and Richard Branson. I was being hired by Virgin. It was my spot. It was unbelievable. The whole thing came true. Now, sidebar, if you don't think I had about 8,000 setbacks between that day that I wrote that vision and actually achieving that vision, oh my God, it was the first six months was a disaster. I mean, all these folks actually could get the way in the angle, but division remained constant. I always had division. I had all these goals. I'm going to go to Asia and speak here and earn ex-dowels, take that hire, this person, very little of that actually happened. See, I always have this saying that makes people laugh. I'll say, there's only one way to get rich in this world and that's quick. You got to get rich quick. Now, I'm not talking about a get rich quick scheme. What I'm saying is that you tend to work and work and work and work and work and not get the result you want and you work and you work and you work and you don't get the result you want. And when you finally do tons and tons of work and that last piece of the puzzle falls, let's bam, then you start hitting it and making a lot of money really fast. Well, that's what happened to me and I had some false starts, but I stayed true to the vision, did the work. And as soon as I saw the way in, took off to the stars. And I actually was within two and a half years, I blew my vision out of it, was making more money, living in a nicer place. I mean, every, I was speaking at all the biggest companies. I had amazing friends. I was back in the stock market again, doing deals in the research, things I never even imagined all came to me. So what did I do? I sat down, said, let me write a new. I need to write a new vision statement. And I started writing about things about, I'm going to start doing charity work in Africa and all this sort of stuff and expanded my vision. I'm going to change things out and start speaking more selectively and coaching young entrepreneur. I did a new vision statement. And again, it became no longer about money because I became wealthy again. So the money aspect, I had all the material stuff, but that doesn't mean I didn't have a new vision. The vision was, you know, what do I do with that? How do I contribute? How do I give back? And how do I keep making more money too? Because there's nothing wrong with that. I wanted to do both at the same time and I wanted to feel great about it every day. You know, who am I allowing myself to work with? I can be selective now. What kind of clients do I want? You know, where do I want to vacation? What do I want to see? What do I do in my spare time? You know, what are the possessions I really want? What do I want to get the charity about? What are the charities I want to be involved with? And all the stuff and on and on and on. I just rewrote my vision. So I wrote one, achieved it and wrote a new one. And I still have one today. All right, you're back and I'm tired. So I've told all my life story like that. It's a bit draining. I'll tell you, I would never do that if she hadn't asked me the question. It kind of prod anatomy, but it's pretty amazing when you when you look at that, it's a testament to really what you can do when you essentially harness not just the outer world of success, meaning being a great closer, having great business skills. But you also couple that with knowing how to harness the inner world of success. Those two together, powerful combination. Now, in this quiz section coming up here, I'm going to really force you to stop pulling out the rest of your vision statement here and make sure you get it down on paper. Your why is fully developed and every stone is basically left unturned. When you're done with that, meet me next lesson. See you soon.
